text: back in the he days of 2002 I was still
a young and optimistic teenager instead
of the horrifying burned out husk of a
man you see today and I was flicking
through the TV channels one day when I
caught a commercial for a new show
called Firefly on the Sci-Fi channel
made by that guy who did Buffy the
Vampire Slayer now I really like Buffy
but I have to admit this Firefly thing
didn't really grab me it looked like
some weird mashup of cowboy movies and
the kind of lowbudget Sci-Fi Action
shows that were all the rage at the time
and I remember thinking I don't get it
this looks weird and silly and I don't
know if I meant to take it seriously or
laugh at it and because I already had
farce gape and Stargate sg1 and Star
Trek Enterprise to keep me occupied
Firefly just kind of passed me by and by
the end of the year it had been canceled
anyway so who cares just another aborted
Sci-Fi show with Grand ideas that never
really got off the grounds guess I
dodged a bullet with that one a
I'm telling you all this because people
like me are the exact reason why Firefly
never found the audience it needed and
got unceremoniously cancelled by a
network that never really believed in it
in the first place but having finally
caved in and watched it with mer like 20
years too late I can honestly say that
is now a genuinely big regret on my part
why because Firefly is a [ __ ] awesome
show with some of the best character
work I've ever seen in a project like
this a fully fleshed Out World of
Adventure and vibrant cultures briming
with with potential right in this light
years ahead of the dull flaky sludge
we're subjected to today a cast of
superb actors at the top of their game
and most importantly a genuine passion
from everyone involved that you can feel
in every single scene and conversation
they really were trying to create
something special with this show Even
though seemingly everything was working
against them and there's something truly
admirable about that kind of Glorious
failure the show is set a few hundred
years into the future where human's
formed a single world government called
the Alliance and spread out into the
Galaxy to colonize nearby star systems
some of these tried to break away from
Alliance control which resulted in a
Galactic Civil War that the separatist
known as brown coats ultimately lost now
the outer colonies exist in an uneasy
peace where technology and culture have
devolved into a kind of wild west
Frontier mentality and Law and Order is
maintained by Whoever has the biggest
guns it's in this world of danger and
possibilities that were introduced to
Malcolm Reynolds a former Brown coat
turned Smuggler and his ragtag crew who
live and work with him aboard the
serenity they spend their days taking on
dangerous jobs that other people won't
do transporting illegal cargo trying to
avoid Alliance patrols and the
cannibalistic space pirates known as
reavers all so that they can scrape
together enough money to keep the beat
up Serenity running but things take a
sudden change for Malcolm and the others
when they're hired by a young doctor to
transport his sister River who's on the
run from the alliance and is well a bit
strange to put it mildly clearly there's
more to her than meets the eye and as
the season unfolds the crew begin to
realize that River might just hold the
key to changeing the balance of power in
the colonies now as I'm describing it
here it probably sounds like a fairly
generic space adventure show the kind of
thing you've probably seen a million
times before but the devil as always is
in the detail and this is where Firefly
really comes into its own first up
there's the characters there's nine
people aboard the serenity all with
their own unique personalities goals and
motivations many of which conflict with
each other at times and their various
perspectives and ideals are what formed
the backbone of Firefly's dramatic Arc
there's Zoe Malcolm second in command an
old war buddy who's always got his back
her husband wash the ship's talented but
slightly flaky pilot kayy the engineer
who somehow manages to keep the serenity
running despite all the punishment that
it takes Jane a gun toen Meathead who's
more interested in profit than teamwork
book a kind of traveling preacher with a
mysterious past inara a highclass escort
who hitches a ride on on the serenity to
get from job to job and has a will they
won't they relationship with Malcolm and
Simon and his sister River who are on
the run from the authorities while
trying to reconnect with each other and
here's the thing every single one of
them is interesting likable fleshed out
and complex you get a good sense of who
everyone is and what matters to them
within like the first half hour of the
first episode but the more you learn
about them the more layers of history
and personality get built up watching
them spark off each other trading quips
and insults react to ideas and
situations in different ways and even
just hanging out and having dinner
together it all just cements the
realization that wow they really did put
a lot of thought into crafting these
characters and if you were to ask me who
my favorite is I'd genuinely struggle to
answer because they all have something
to recommend them Jane is hilarious with
his simple-minded pursuit of profit and
gratification never passing up a chance
to indulge himself but there's also
darker moments where he's actually
forced to confront the Fallout of his
actions and you can see how much it
shakes him Malcolm's the consumate
leader always cool Under Pressure smart
and calculating but also free-spirited
and adventurous willing to take risks
with his ship and crew but fanatically
loyal to the people under his commands
and believe me he is not a man to be
trifled with if you turn against him
holy [ __ ] a smart capable and brave male
lead who's also respected by his crew
you can tell this show wasn't made today
that's for [ __ ] sure and he's played
to absolute perfection by Nathan fillian
who apparently considers Mal the best
role of his career and frankly it's
pretty tough to argue with it then
there's Kaye who I genuinely think might
be one of the most lovable characters
I've ever seen on TV she's a brilliantly
talented engineer who can fix almost
anything but she's also feminine and
endearing and well nice her quarters
look like a teenage girl's bedroom and
in one episode where she's forced to
wear a dress to attend a formal ball
she's delighted by the simple joy of
just dressing up and looking pretty if
this was written today she'd be some big
Burly lesbian with a crew in a bad
attitude obnoxiously complaining about
how dumb the men around her are we
really did not appreciate how good we
had it back in the day but it's exactly
this kind of nuanced character work the
ability to constantly surprise the
viewer without ever feeling forced or
mean-spirited that helps to remind me
that yes Jos Weeden used to be really
[ __ ] good at this kind of thing he
gets a lot of flack nowadays for
basically being the instigator of the
cring inducing Marvel humor and I've
probably even done it myself but I think
that's doing the guy a dis service
because most of the time it's not really
his work that's the problem it's writers
of lesser Talent awkwardly trying to
imitate him to the point of parody
Weeden himself once described this show
as nine people staring out into the
darkness of space and seeing nine
different things and really I think
that's the perfect summation of what
Firefly represents it's a show about
perspectives different ideas and
mindsets and how they conflict and work
together and of course I can't talk
about the characters without discussing
the serenity herself which is basically
the show's 10th character she's a beat
up old freighter a bit like the
millennian Falcon designed not just as a
vehicle but as a home for the crew and
it has what I can only describe as a
coziness to it that was so unique to
that particular time in entertainment
nowadays most sets are designed to be
dark and Moody and cool and they all
come across as so Stark and nondescript
that they're almost depressing to look
at the serenity is the exact opposite
cobbled together and lived in and Rough
and Ready there's little details and
Easter eggs everywhere the sets were all
designed to connect together because we
didn't really wanted the audience to
know the layout of the ship and where
everyone was at any given time and it's
this desire to create a usable
believable space that just adds to the
feeling of a show that had a lot of love
and care put into it obviously not
everything's going to hold up quite as
well as the writing this was a show made
over 20 years ago on a not particularly
big budget so needless to say the VFX
are pretty janky by today's standards
there's a lot of recycled locations and
props many the oneandone sets look
pretty Bare Bones and sometimes the
action set pieces are reaching for
something that's a bit beyond their
grasp but you know what I don't give a
[ __ ] I'd take a Rough and Ready show
like this over some visually spectacular
piece of soulless corporatized trash any
day of the [ __ ] weak ultimately
Firefly's unique Fusion of sci-fi space
adventure with big thematic overtones
and down in dirty Wild West Aesthetics
with gunfights and bar brawls and old
timey dialogue was definitely a bold
move from a creative standpoint but in
retrospect it was probably a bit too far
ahead of its time for people to really
understand what it was going for this
problem combined with a pretty crap time
slot and a studio that never fully got
behind it meant that Firefly never found
the audience that it deserved fan
petitions and online campaigns
eventually persuaded the studio to make
Serenity finally giv Weeden the budget
and resources to bring his vision to
life on the big screen but even that
wasn't enough to save the show and it
was consigned to history after just 14
episodes and then that truly does suck
because I feel like the whole history of
this show was dogged by those two most
terrible words if only if only the
studio had gotten behind it more if only
idiots like me had been open-minded
enough to give it a chance back in 2002
if only they'd stuck with it for another
season if only there hadn't been so much
competition if only and when I think
about all the absolute dog [ __ ]
brainless soulless pointless TV shows we
get bombarded with today the somehow
keep getting renewed for season after
season that spend more on a single
episode than Firefly got for its entire
run it makes me genuinely sad because
little by little we're losing that
improvisational Spirit and the Creative
Drive that made shows like this possible
in the first place we really didn't
appreciate how good we had it back then
and probably never will again and
ultimately Firefly is just another sad
example of wasted potential of missed
opportunities of what might have been so
you know what Firefly on behalf of
myself and everyone else who should have
believed in you but
didn't I'm sorry you deserve better than
you got and in the
ends we're the ones who lost
out
anyway that's all I've got for
today go away now
